title: Genmab — Hematology — Thesis
html:
Genmab Hematology Thesis Tracker — Vinoth's POV

TA: DLBCL / Follicular Lymphoma (EPKINLY / epcoritamab) Initiated: 2026-04-24 Last reviewed: 2026-04-24 Review cadence: Quarterly (next: July 2026)

Thesis 1: The FL Combo Approval Is the Real Growth Driver — Not DLBCL Monotherapy

The bet: Epkinly's November 2025 FDA approval for epcoritamab + R2 in 2L+ FL will be the primary revenue growth driver through 2026-2027, not the original DLBCL 3L+ monotherapy indication. FL is a larger, more durable market with longer treatment duration, and the combo data (ORR/CR 96%/~75%, significantly superior PFS vs R2 alone) is far more compelling than the DLBCL monotherapy data. The FL combo will drive the majority of new starts and revenue acceleration from here.

Why this matters for Vinoth: Genmab's study priorities should shift toward measuring FL combo adoption and FL-specific MSL effectiveness. The current MSL study and PET tracker are DLBCL-centric (reflecting the original launch indication). If FL becomes the dominant revenue source, ZoomRx's study design needs to rebalance. This is a BD opportunity to propose a dedicated FL bispecific adoption tracker.

Evidence FOR:

  • EPCORE FL-1 data: ~3 out of 4 patients achieved CR; significantly superior PFS — this is category-changing data
  • NCCN Category 1 Preferred designation for epcoritamab + R2 in 2L FL (fastest possible guideline uptake)
  • FL patient pool is larger than 3L+ DLBCL and patients stay on therapy longer (up to 12 cycles of combination)
  • FL R2 backbone is well-understood — physicians are comfortable adding epcoritamab to a familiar regimen
  • Genmab FY2025 Epkinly revenue: $468M (+67%) — growth accelerated post-FL approval in Q4 2025

Evidence AGAINST:

  • Breyanzi (CAR-T) in FL has ORR 96%, CR 73% — near-identical response rates; some HCPs may prefer one-time CAR-T
  • CELESTIMO (mosunetuzumab + lenalidomide) is a direct competitor in the same FL niche — if it reads out positively, Roche has parity
  • FL patients are often managed by community oncologists who may be slower to adopt bispecific step-up dosing
  • Epcoritamab + R2 requires 12 cycles; the complexity of triple-drug regimen + step-up may limit community uptake initially

Confidence: High. The data is the strongest in epcoritamab's portfolio and the NCCN designation accelerates adoption.

What to watch: Q1-Q2 2026 Epkinly revenue breakout (FL vs DLBCL starts if Genmab discloses). CELESTIMO readout timing. ZoomRx MSL study FL-specific findings.

Thesis 2: Epcoritamab's 2L DLBCL Positioning Is Vulnerable — STARGLO Gives Glofitamab the Edge

The bet: Despite EPCORE DLBCL-1 showing a PFS benefit (HR 0.74) for epcoritamab monotherapy in 2L+ R/R DLBCL, the failure to demonstrate OS benefit (HR 0.96) weakens the case for a 2L label expansion. Meanwhile, glofitamab + GemOx (STARGLO) demonstrated a statistically significant 41% reduction in death risk (HR 0.59, OS benefit) in 2L R/R DLBCL. In the 2L DLBCL space specifically, glofitamab currently has the superior dataset. Epcoritamab's 2L DLBCL positioning depends on combination data (EPCORE DLBCL-2 with R-CHOP) rather than monotherapy.

Why this matters for Vinoth: When tracking competitive messaging for Genmab, the narrative needs to be carefully segmented. In 3L+ DLBCL, epcoritamab has a solid monotherapy position. In FL, epcoritamab + R2 is dominant. But in 2L DLBCL, glofitamab + GemOx has the data advantage. ZoomRx study designs for Genmab should reflect this nuance — don't lump all DLBCL lines together.

Evidence FOR (that 2L is vulnerable):

  • EPCORE DLBCL-1: PFS HR 0.74 is positive but OS HR 0.96 is clearly negative — mixed Phase 3 result
  • STARGLO: Glofitamab + GemOx OS HR 0.59 (p=0.011) — statistically significant OS benefit in 2L R/R DLBCL
  • Genmab acknowledged "zero read across" from EPCORE DLBCL-1 monotherapy to the ongoing combination trials — implicitly acknowledging monotherapy limitations
  • CAR-T (Yescarta, Breyanzi) remain the dominant 2L preference for fit patients

Evidence AGAINST (epcoritamab still has a path):

  • EPCORE DLBCL-2 (epcoritamab + R-CHOP in 1L DLBCL) could leapfrog 2L entirely if positive — readout expected 2026
  • SubQ convenience gives epcoritamab a community adoption edge even with inferior efficacy data in 2L
  • EPCORE NHL-2 Arm 1 (epcoritamab + R-CHOP) showed 98% ORR, 85% CR in 1L — early combination data is strong
  • 73% of EPCORE DLBCL-1 patients had 2+ prior lines — the 2L-specific subset may show different results when data is fully presented

Confidence: Medium-high that 2L DLBCL is a competitive weakness for epcoritamab monotherapy. High confidence that the combination strategy (EPCORE DLBCL-2) is the path to competitiveness.

What to watch: EPCORE DLBCL-1 full data presentation at a medical meeting. EPCORE DLBCL-2 Phase 3 readout. Glofitamab + GemOx real-world adoption post-STARGLO approval.

Thesis 3: The Bispecific vs CAR-T Debate Favors Bispecifics Long-Term — But Only in Community Settings

The bet: Bispecific antibodies (epcoritamab, glofitamab) will gradually erode CAR-T market share, but only in community oncology settings where CAR-T access is limited. In academic centers, CAR-T will remain preferred for fit patients due to deeper responses and one-time administration. The market will bifurcate: academic centers remain CAR-T-first, community practices become bispecific-first. Epcoritamab's SubQ advantage positions it as the lead bispecific in the community segment.

Why this matters for Vinoth: ZoomRx's sampling strategy must differentiate between academic and community oncologists. The Q4 2023 PET data shows YESCARTA had 81% prescribing intent vs EPKINLY's 67% — but this likely over-represents academic prescribers. MSL data (Wave 1) shows epcoritamab MSL reach was significantly higher in community hospitals. The community adoption story is epcoritamab's primary growth vector.

Evidence FOR:

  • MSL Wave 1: Epcoritamab MSL reach significantly higher vs competitors, driven by community hospital reach
  • SubQ administration enables outpatient dosing after step-up — fundamental community advantage
  • CAR-T requires REMS-certified centers (predominantly academic); bispecifics do not
  • Epcoritamab shows activity in CAR-T exposed patients (ORR 52%) — can be used after CAR-T failure as well as instead of CAR-T
  • Genmab FY2025 earnings highlight "increased patient access" as a growth driver

Evidence AGAINST:

  • PET tracker Q4 2023: YESCARTA prescribing intent (81%) >> EPKINLY (67%) — CAR-T converts better when available
  • CAR-T provides one-time treatment vs indefinite bispecific therapy — patients and payers may prefer the finality
  • Glofitamab's fixed-duration offers a middle ground that also appeals to community settings
  • Step-up dosing for epcoritamab still requires initial inpatient/monitored setting — not fully "office-based" yet

Confidence: Medium. The bifurcation hypothesis is logical but unquantified. No published data segments bispecific vs CAR-T adoption by practice setting.

What to watch: MSL Wave 2 data (community vs academic splits). Any Genmab/AbbVie earnings commentary on community adoption metrics. Real-world data on epcoritamab new starts by site type.

Thesis 4: MSL Effectiveness Is a Measurable Competitive Advantage — and ZoomRx Is Proving It

The bet: The AbbVie/Genmab MSL team is outperforming Roche/Genentech and BMS MSLs on key metrics, and this advantage is translatable to commercial outcomes. The MSL Wave 1 data shows AbbVie/Genmab MSLs driving the strongest manufacturer perceptions, highest "Best in Class" ratings, and ~60% high-impact interaction rate. If this MSL advantage correlates with prescribing behavior, it validates the entire MSL effectiveness study as a commercial ROI tool — which is exactly what ZoomRx should be selling to Genmab.

Why this matters for Vinoth: This is a direct BD lever. If ZoomRx can demonstrate that MSL effectiveness metrics (from our study) correlate with prescription intent or new starts, we can position the MSL study as a must-have commercial optimization tool, not just a nice-to-have measurement. The Wave 1 data already shows: AbbVie/Genmab MSLs drove strongest perception change, highest "Best in Class" ratings, and more peer discussion post-interaction. The next step is linking this to actual prescribing.

Evidence FOR:

  • MSL Wave 1: AbbVie/Genmab MSLs rated most often as "Best in Class" across all three companies
  • ~60% of epcoritamab MSL interactions were high-impact (leading vs competitors)
  • Post-MSL interaction, HCPs significantly more likely to research epcoritamab independently and attend events vs competitors
  • Epcoritamab FL narratives had strong recall and effectiveness; DLBCL narratives outperformed study averages
  • AbbVie/Genmab MSL interactions improved FL knowledge more than DLBCL knowledge — aligning with the FL growth thesis

Evidence AGAINST:

  • BMS MSLs led in "trusted partner" perception — relationship quality matters, not just message delivery
  • Roche/Genentech MSLs had comparable effectiveness for glofitamab (75% vs 74% for epcoritamab)
  • No causal link established between MSL effectiveness and prescribing behavior in the current study design
  • Small sample sizes throughout (n=30 per company) — directional, not statistically powered

Confidence: High that the MSL advantage is real. Medium that it's commercially translatable without additional study.

What to watch: MSL Wave 2 data. Any correlation analysis between MSL interaction quality and downstream prescribing changes. Genmab's appetite for continued MSL investment.

Thesis 5: The 1L DLBCL Prize (EPCORE DLBCL-2) Is the Franchise-Defining Catalyst

The bet: If epcoritamab + R-CHOP beats R-CHOP in frontline DLBCL (EPCORE DLBCL-2, ~900 patients, readout expected 2026), it transforms Epkinly from a late-line niche product into a frontline standard-of-care component — comparable to what Pola-R-CHP (POLARIX) achieved for POLIVY. This would be the single largest TAM expansion event for Epkinly, potentially making it a multi-billion-dollar drug. The early Phase 1/2 data (98% ORR, 85% CR) is extraordinarily strong.

Why this matters for Vinoth: A positive EPCORE DLBCL-2 readout would fundamentally change Genmab's study needs. Instead of tracking late-line bispecific adoption, ZoomRx would need to track frontline DLBCL treatment pattern shifts, 1L competitive dynamics (vs Pola-R-CHP), and the impact on the entire downstream treatment sequence. This is a "when, not if" BD planning moment — start preparing the 1L DLBCL measurement framework now so ZoomRx is ready to pitch when the data drops.

Evidence FOR:

  • EPCORE NHL-2 Arm 1: ORR 98%, CR 85%; 92% remained in CR at 2 years; 74% at 3 years — numerically outstanding
  • Efficacy maintained across high-risk subgroups (high IPI, bulky disease, cell of origin)
  • If the 85% CR rate holds in the Phase 3, it would be the highest CR rate ever reported in 1L DLBCL
  • Genmab expects readout in 2026 — catalyst is imminent
  • SKYGLO (glofitamab competitor in 1L) is also ongoing — validates industry conviction that bispecific + chemo in 1L is the next frontier

Evidence AGAINST:

  • Phase 1/2 (n=47) to Phase 3 (~900) efficacy attrition is common — the 85% CR may not replicate at scale
  • Pola-R-CHP (POLIVY) is already established in 1L DLBCL — displacing an entrenched treatment requires overwhelmingly superior data
  • Adding a bispecific to R-CHOP increases complexity: step-up dosing in cycle 1, CRS monitoring, longer treatment duration
  • R-CHOP alone cures ~60% of DLBCL patients — the incremental benefit of adding epcoritamab may be modest for the majority who would have been cured anyway
  • SKYGLO (glofitamab + Pola-R-CHP) targets the same 1L space — two bispecific competitors in 1L simultaneously

Confidence: Medium-high that epcoritamab + R-CHOP will show a PFS benefit. Medium that the magnitude of benefit will be practice-changing. The CR rate data from Phase 1/2 is the strongest signal in the entire bispecific landscape.

What to watch: EPCORE DLBCL-2 readout timing and topline data. SKYGLO readout. Genmab earnings commentary on 1L DLBCL strategy.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

NCCN Category 1 Preferred designation for epcoritamab + R2 in 2L FL (fastest possible guideline uptake)
